atoms.semantic_claim: The engine tree module imports Ritual metrics so canonical-chain and block-acceptance telemetry can be emitted from tree/mod.rs.

destinations.snippet: The destination now depends on `ritual-metrics` and uses fully qualified `ritual_metrics::...` call sites from the engine tree and payload validator.
destinations.evidence: Post-fix search after commit `4af480689a` found engine-tree and payload-validator `ritual_metrics` call sites.

destinations.snippet: ritual_metrics is used in the nodebuilder executor for payload execution phases, not in engine tree acceptance.
destinations.evidence: This is a related destination use of ritual_metrics but does not cover the source file's tree/mod.rs import or call sites.

atoms.notes: Covered by commit `4af480689a`; the port uses fully qualified calls rather than a standalone `use ritual_metrics;` import.

atoms.source_snippet: MIN_BLOCKS_FOR_PIPELINE_RUN changes from EPOCH_SLOTS to 64 with rationale about pipeline execution time, batch efficiency, live-sync coverage, and memory utilization.
atoms.semantic_claim: Backfill pipeline sync should not be triggered until the live-sync gap exceeds 64 blocks, replacing the epoch-slot threshold with a Ritual-tuned threshold and documented rationale.

destinations.snippet: Destination now declares `pub(crate) const MIN_BLOCKS_FOR_PIPELINE_RUN: u64 = 64;` and applies it in the backfill threshold check.
destinations.evidence: Post-fix search after commit `4af480689a` found the fixed 64-block threshold in the destination engine tree.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Drive forkchoice gaps of 33, 64, and 65 blocks and assert only the >64 gap starts pipeline backfill while smaller gaps remain in live block download/execution.
atoms.notes: Covered by commit `4af480689a`; e2e test constants may still have comments describing the historical EPOCH_SLOTS value, but the engine-tree policy constant is now 64.

atoms.source_snippet: After setting the tree canonical head, the patch calls ritual_metrics::block::log_el_update_head(&tip.hash(), tip.number(), tip.timestamp()).
atoms.semantic_claim: Every canonical-chain update records the execution-layer head hash, number, and timestamp through Ritual block metrics before notifications are emitted.

destinations.snippet: Destination now calls `ritual_metrics::block::log_el_update_head(&tip.hash(), tip.number(), tip.timestamp())` in `on_canonical_chain_update`.
destinations.evidence: Post-fix search after commit `4af480689a` found the `log_el_update_head` call in the destination engine tree.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Commit and reorg canonical chains and assert a Ritual EL head metric/log is emitted with the new tip hash, number, and timestamp for each update.

atoms.source_snippet: The patch calls ritual_metrics::tx::log_tx_finalized_chain_update(&chain_update) after reorg handling and before canonical_in_memory_state.update_chain.
atoms.semantic_claim: Canonical commit and reorg updates emit Ritual transaction finalization metrics for the complete NewCanonicalChain update.

destinations.snippet: Destination now calls `ritual_metrics::tx::log_tx_finalized_chain_update(&chain_update)` after reorg reinsertion and before in-memory canonical updates.
destinations.evidence: Post-fix search after commit `4af480689a` found the `log_tx_finalized_chain_update` call in the destination engine tree.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Apply both Commit and Reorg canonical updates containing transactions and assert Ritual finalized-chain transaction metrics are emitted once per update.

atoms.source_snippet: For NewCanonicalChain::Commit and ::Reorg, the patch iterates new executed blocks and logs target ritual::spc::provider with block_number, block_hash, timestamp, and message `Updated header cache with canonical block`.
atoms.semantic_claim: Canonical updates emit per-block Ritual SPC provider debug evidence for each newly canonical block, even though the historical header cache has been removed.

destinations.snippet: Destination has no ritual::spc::provider debug loop in canonical-chain updates.
destinations.evidence: Exact searches for ritual::spc::provider and `Updated header cache with canonical block` across destination repos found no matches.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Canonicalize a multi-block commit and reorg and assert per-new-block SPC provider debug evidence includes block number, hash, and timestamp.
atoms.notes: Telemetry-only, but it changes operational evidence around canonical blocks used by Ritual verification flows.

atoms.source_snippet: insert_block captures block_hash_str, parent_hash_str, tx_count, then logs block_accept_state_provider_fetch as skipped with reason missing_parent_state when state_provider_builder returns None.
atoms.semantic_claim: Disconnected blocks with missing parent state emit a Ritual block-acceptance state-provider metric with skipped status and missing_parent_state reason before buffering the block.

destinations.snippet: Destination logs `block_accept_state_provider_fetch` with skipped status and `missing_parent_state` reason when the parent state provider is unavailable.
destinations.evidence: Post-fix search after commit `4af480689a` found the metric in `payload_validator.rs`; the placement moved from tree/mod.rs because v2.2 delegates block validation through the payload validator.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Submit a payload whose parent state is unavailable and assert the block is buffered as disconnected and a skipped block_accept_state_provider_fetch metric with reason missing_parent_state is emitted.
atoms.notes: Covered by commit `4af480689a` in the refactored v2.2 payload-validator path.

atoms.source_snippet: validate_header_against_parent is timed with block_accept_consensus_validation; errors log status error with reason, success logs status ok.
atoms.semantic_claim: Parent-header consensus validation during block acceptance emits Ritual timing metrics for both success and failure outcomes.

destinations.snippet: Destination logs `block_accept_consensus_validation` around consensus and parent-header validation success and error paths.
destinations.evidence: Post-fix search after commit `4af480689a` found `block_accept_consensus_validation` in `payload_validator.rs`; the placement is structurally equivalent under the v2.2 refactor.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Submit one block with a valid parent header and one with a parent-consensus mismatch; assert ok and error block_accept_consensus_validation metrics include block/parent hashes, tx count, and failure reason.
atoms.notes: Covered by commit `4af480689a` in the refactored v2.2 payload-validator path.

atoms.source_snippet: provider_builder.build()? is timed and logs block_accept_state_provider_fetch with status ok.
atoms.semantic_claim: Successful state-provider construction during block acceptance emits a Ritual timing metric separate from the missing-parent skipped case.

destinations.snippet: Destination logs `block_accept_state_provider_fetch` with ok status after successful provider construction.
destinations.evidence: Post-fix search after commit `4af480689a` found the ok state-provider fetch phase metric in `payload_validator.rs`.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Insert a connected block and assert successful state provider construction emits block_accept_state_provider_fetch with ok status and expected block metadata.
atoms.notes: Covered by commit `4af480689a` in the refactored v2.2 payload-validator path.

atoms.source_snippet: compute_trie_input logs block_accept_trie_input with ok status; when parallel state root is disabled, it logs skipped with reason parallel_state_root_disabled.
atoms.semantic_claim: Trie-input preparation during block acceptance emits Ritual timing metrics for computed and skipped paths, distinguishing parallel-state-root-disabled skips.

destinations.snippet: Destination tree contains trie/change-set handling but no block_accept_trie_input ok or skipped Ritual phase metrics.
destinations.evidence: Exact searches found no block_accept_trie_input or parallel_state_root_disabled metric reason in destination repos.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Run block insertion with parallel state root enabled and disabled; assert block_accept_trie_input emits ok for computed trie input and skipped/parallel_state_root_disabled for the disabled path.
atoms.notes: No equivalent trie-input acceptance telemetry was found in the port or dependency repos.

atoms.source_snippet: The executor.execute_metered call is timed with ritual_metrics::block::log_block_phase("block_accept_execution", ..., "ok").
atoms.semantic_claim: Successful block execution during engine-tree block acceptance emits a Ritual block_accept_execution timing metric around the executor call.

destinations.snippet: Destination logs `block_accept_execution` with ok and error outcomes around block execution.
destinations.evidence: Post-fix search after commit `4af480689a` found `block_accept_execution` in `payload_validator.rs`.

destinations.snippet: Nodebuilder emits block_execute_registry_update, block_execute_setup, block_execute_transactions, block_execute_consensus_verification, and block_execute_finalization.
destinations.evidence: These are related executor-internal metrics, but they do not provide the source engine-tree block_accept_execution envelope or label.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Insert a valid payload and assert an engine-tree block_accept_execution metric wraps the executor call with block hash, parent hash, tx count, and ok status.
atoms.notes: Covered by commit `4af480689a` in the refactored v2.2 payload-validator path.

atoms.source_snippet: validate_block_post_execution logs block_accept_post_exec_consensus_validation with error reason on failure and ok on success.
atoms.semantic_claim: Post-execution consensus validation emits Ritual block-acceptance timing metrics for both valid output and invalid output error paths.

destinations.snippet: Destination logs `block_accept_post_exec_consensus_validation` with ok and error outcomes around post-execution consensus validation.
destinations.evidence: Post-fix search after commit `4af480689a` found `block_accept_post_exec_consensus_validation` in `payload_validator.rs`.

destinations.snippet: Nodebuilder logs a different executor-internal consensus verification phase.
destinations.evidence: The label and placement differ and do not cover validate_block_post_execution in the tree acceptance path.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Force post-execution consensus validation failure and assert block_accept_post_exec_consensus_validation emits error with the validation reason before invalid-block handling.
atoms.notes: Covered by commit `4af480689a` in the refactored v2.2 payload-validator path.

atoms.source_snippet: payload_validator.validate_block_post_execution_with_hashed_state logs block_accept_ritual_validation with error reason on failure and ok on success.
atoms.semantic_claim: Ritual post-execution payload validation emits explicit block-acceptance timing metrics for success and failure paths.

destinations.snippet: Destination logs `block_accept_ritual_validation` with ok and error outcomes around `validate_block_post_execution_with_hashed_state`.
destinations.evidence: Post-fix search after commit `4af480689a` found `block_accept_ritual_validation` in `payload_validator.rs`.

destinations.snippet: Nodebuilder wires a Ritual post-execution validator, but not the source tree-level Ritual validation metric.
destinations.evidence: This is validation plumbing, not equivalent telemetry for success/error validation outcomes.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Trigger a Ritual post-execution validation rejection and assert block_accept_ritual_validation emits error status with the rejection reason; verify a valid block emits ok.
atoms.notes: Covered by commit `4af480689a` in the refactored v2.2 payload-validator path.

atoms.source_snippet: The patch records used_parallel_state_root = maybe_state_root.is_some() and logs block_accept_state_root with status ok for parallel root or fallback with reason parallel_state_root_unavailable.
atoms.semantic_claim: State-root calculation emits Ritual acceptance telemetry that distinguishes parallel state root success from serial fallback.

destinations.snippet: Destination logs `block_accept_state_root` with ok or fallback status and `parallel_state_root_unavailable` reason for fallback.
destinations.evidence: Post-fix search after commit `4af480689a` found `block_accept_state_root` and `parallel_state_root_unavailable` in `payload_validator.rs`.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: Exercise block insertion with parallel state-root available and unavailable, then assert block_accept_state_root emits ok for parallel output and fallback/parallel_state_root_unavailable for serial calculation.
atoms.notes: Covered by commit `4af480689a` in the refactored v2.2 payload-validator path.

atoms.source_snippet: Under cfg(feature = async-context), InsertBlockErrorKind::Execution(BlockExecutionError::Validation(BlockValidationError::OnnxModelUnavailableForVerification { .. })) warns and returns PayloadStatusEnum::Syncing before invalid-header cache insertion.
atoms.semantic_claim: Temporary ONNX model unavailability during verification is treated as SYNCING and is not cached as INVALID, allowing the block to be retried after model download/backfill.

destinations.snippet: Destination now has an explicit `OnnxModelUnavailableForVerification` branch in `on_insert_block_error` that returns `PayloadStatusEnum::Syncing` before invalid-header insertion.
destinations.evidence: Post-fix search after commit `4af480689a` found the explicit ONNX-unavailable-to-SYNCING branch in the destination engine tree.

destinations.snippet: Payload building still skips transactions for OnnxModelUnavailableForVerification; engine-tree new-payload verification status handling is now covered separately.
destinations.evidence: This remains a related builder path, while commit `4af480689a` covers engine-tree `SYNCING` status behavior.

destinations.snippet: The dependency still produces BlockValidationError::OnnxModelUnavailableForVerification during verification.
destinations.evidence: The error source exists, and the destination engine tree now maps it to SYNCING/no-invalid-cache semantics.

atoms.negative_test_candidate: With async-context enabled, make verification raise OnnxModelUnavailableForVerification during new payload handling and assert the response is SYNCING, invalid_headers is not updated, and a later retry can validate the same block.
atoms.notes: Covered by commit `4af480689a`; localnet ONNX traffic validation passed after the change.

summary: Partially resolved after commit `4af480689a fix: restore engine tree ritual metrics parity`. The fixed 64-block threshold, Ritual metrics crate plumbing, EL head/finalized transaction metrics, most block_accept phase metrics, state-root fallback metric, and ONNX-unavailable-as-SYNCING behavior are now covered. Two telemetry atoms remain open: per-canonical-block `ritual::spc::provider` debug evidence and `block_accept_trie_input` / `parallel_state_root_disabled` trie-input telemetry.
notes: Post-fix verification searched the current v2.2 port after commit `4af480689a`. Covered atoms may live in `crates/engine/tree/src/tree/payload_validator.rs` rather than the original downstream `tree/mod.rs` hunk because v2.2 moved block validation responsibilities behind the payload validator. Remaining missing atoms are telemetry-only but still tracked for explicit parity or scope decision.
